what_it_does: This file is a ledger metadata stub for a design-source image asset; it records an id, address, blob reference, MIME type, provenance, author session, and creation timestamp for recent-pitches/p-38.jpg.
observations: YAML frontmatter defines an image ledger entry with id recent-pitches/p-38 and address image://design-source/recent-pitches/p-38
References a blob via blob://b2:e1a6559482dca2c84cc455c9b95bdb42 with mime image/jpeg
Records w:0 and h:0 despite being an image
Lists source as design_source_material and author_session as ch-3mpkjg3r
Body text states 'SOURCE design ground-truth — recent-pitches/p-38.jpg'
standouts: Width and height are recorded as 0 for a JPEG image
The entry is explicitly labeled as SOURCE design ground-truth
Asset is addressed with image:// and stored via blob://b2
conventions: Uses YAML frontmatter for structured ledger metadata
Addresses assets with a channel-scoped URI scheme (image://design-source/...)
References content by a blob hash
Includes provenance fields: source, author_session, created
concerns: Image dimensions are zero, which is inconsistent with an actual image/jpeg asset
No descriptive text, alt text, or content summary for the image
links array is empty, so no related assets or derivations are recorded
notes: A sparse provenance stub for a design-source pitch image that identifies a blob and author but carries no pixel dimensions or content description.
questions: Why are the width and height values 0 for an image asset?
What does the pitch image actually depict?
What is the meaning or scope of author_session ch-3mpkjg3r?
Is the referenced blob resolvable and does its hash match the actual file?
purpose_vs_actual: Stated purpose is to serve as SOURCE design ground-truth metadata for a pitch image; actual content is a minimal stub that records a blob and provenance but lacks dimensions, description, or links, so it only partially fulfills a ground-truth role.
intention_signals: It appears to be trying to register and anchor a design pitch image in a persistent, addressable ledger so its origin, creation time, and content blob can be tracked.
novelty: The use of blob://b2 and image:// addressing for a non-code design asset within a code/design substrate ledger.
contribution: It contributes a stable, addressable metadata record that maps a design-source image path to a content blob, author session, and creation timestamp, supporting asset provenance and retrieval.
summary_for_embedding: A ledger metadata stub for the design-source JPEG image recent-pitches/p-38, referencing blob b2:e1a6559482dca2c84cc455c9b95bdb42, with zero recorded dimensions and a creation timestamp.
intention_for_embedding: To record provenance and provide an addressable catalog entry for a design-source pitch image.
